reviews.review_text:
This is our first day here, so hopefully there’s nothing else that changes our opinion, but unfortunately the shower blocks need serious attention.

The men’s and women’s setup itself is fine, but the cleanliness was honestly some of the worst we’ve seen. One shower was occupied, the next was covered in sand and hair, and by the time I walked further down the block, the main drain at the end appeared almost completely blocked. The last few showers basically couldn’t even be used unless you wanted to stand in dirty old water.

The shower I ended up using still had hair on the walls, water and grime all over the bench where you’re supposed to put your clean clothes, sand everywhere and very poor drainage. If someone in a higher shower is washing off, their dirty water literally runs down onto your feet while you’re trying to shower. You would honestly be crazy not to wear thongs in there.

The shower heads themselves also need maintenance, and overall it just feels like the amenities aren’t being properly looked after anymore. We’ve heard this used to be a really good caravan park, which makes it even more disappointing.

This honestly feels like something that could be massively improved with regular maintenance and a proper pressure clean of the shower blocks. At the moment, the cleanliness of the amenities is nowhere near the standard you’d expect. Hoping the rest of our stay is better.

Ja, die Bäder sind etwas in die Jahre gekommen. Aber ansonsten hat uns der Platz sehr gut gefallen und hatten auch kein Problem mit Mäusen oder ähnliches.
Toll ist die Nähe zum Strand und die Bade-/Schnorchelmöglichkeiten. Zudem hatten wir bombastisches Wetter und haben es in vollen Zügen genossen.

We stayed in a Park Villa - positives: it was spacious, hot shower, air con, nice patio, full kitchen, mostly clean & great location. We did not see or hear any type of mouse activity like other reviews suggests.
Negatives: the bed and pillows were rock hard and I wouldn't stay again because of this. The bathroom shower needs a deep clean or replacement as there is inches of built up soap scum etc. around the door and base. For $400 a night I think these two things should not be a problem. Beds & pillows should be replaced and shower sorted.

Worst caravan park after 7 weeks traveling in Australia - very expensive for no value at all: extremely dirty showers, toilets and dishwashing sinks that make you wonder if they are cleaned at all from time to time.
Great kid friendly park in an awesome location, such a boujee resort vibe

Could not recommend avoiding this place enough got put over the other side of town  miles away from facilities
And the main facilities where mould ridden and disgusting

Had to sell an organ to stay and there is no water hookup for caravans horrible experience and terrible caravan park

Not family friendly

Friendly welcome at check-in, which was quick and straightforward. Parking is available right in front of the Bay Lodge room.

The room size was okay, with one queen and one single bed. It comes with a fridge, microwave, gas stove, as well as cutlery, pans, and other kitchen utensils. However, the drawers and utensils felt a bit dirty, so we didn’t feel comfortable using them. The balcony was also a bit unclean, with slightly sticky chairs.

The bathroom is on the smaller side but functional, although some fixtures showed signs of rust, and parts of the insect screen were damaged. The TV seemed very outdated and didn’t work at all (satellite issues I assume). The mattress was somewhat uncomfortable, and the blanket and bedding were of rather low quality.

The overall property is quite large and offers everything you need, including 2-3 restaurants, a bakery, shops, and a grocery store. One downside is that there is no WiFi in the rooms, only at the Reef Café, which is a bit inconvenient.

Nice place. Stayed 3 nights.

Only reason for 1 less star is because they charge $1 per 10 litres of water (20litres free) and the lady in front of me was supposed to pay $14 for her 340 litres but the receptionist said “oh I’ll give it to you for $10” but then when it was my turn, I had to pay full price, no discount. I don’t mind paying for water but at least treat everyone the same and don’t give out free discounts willy nilly…

After readings previous reviews we were slightly apprehensive but we had a wonderful stay here- the facilities were great, everything was clean and the staff were really helpful. There’s even free drinking water on site which we’ve not seen at any other campsites. A great camping spot close to a lovely beach.

It wasn’t terrible but it wasn’t great. We luckily did not experience the mice problem , nor did our neighbours who had been there for a week before. We were worried about the kitchens, but they turned out to be okay and relatively clean. Had a pool too which was nice. However the bathrooms were pretty poor. Apart from the fact they were outdated they didn’t feel the cleanest and occasionally had a few cockroaches. The toilets near the laundry area were nicer than the toilet block nearest to our camp spot. The salty bore water showers didn’t turn out to be too bad but obviously if you’re paying that much money for a campsite you’d hope there would be non potable water in the showers. This also wasn’t available next to your campspot. You had to go to reception to get your free 20L of non potable water. The groundskeepers were very nice , especially the one from Manchester ( sorry we didn’t catch his name!), so no complaints in terms of customer interactions!

Overall it was alright but we were disappointed with the money we paid for it.

I’ve never reviewed a place but thought I would for this park, We watched an older male paint one hand rail and it took him all day. It was Maximum a 30 minute job. He had more smoke breaks than he did work and it blew into our room. The up keep of the rooms is very poor. I’ve added a photo as just one example. It doesn’t take much to make a room look half decent. Although the room was very clean with great views. And the customer service was great. It’s in a perfect location also.
Good place

Would not recommend this campsite. It was quite expensive and not hygienic. The bathrooms had birds nests in them and the kitchen was full of flies and no door to keep them out. The grass was too long where we had to park and the sprinklers were in our way when we arrived. We asked the staff what to do and they were clueless. We had to get wet and move the sprinkler ourselves to be able to park. The showers also use salt water so we couldn’t even have a clean shower. Because of this we also couldn’t wash any good clothes in the washing machines assuming they also use salt water.

Nette kamer met prima Air contionering. Het gehele complex is heel groot en omvat sta plaatsen voor campers en drie niveaus van onderkomens. Wij zaten in de middelste categorie.

Wel wat eigenaardigheden: koud water is te heet om onder te douchen ! , er is geen Wifi op het complex ! Het heet dan wel Bayview maar die View is er voor 90 % van de gasten niet meer want daar staan 10 2 verdieping hoger appartementen. Dit is misleidend en onnodig. Een andere plaatsing had het zicht op zee veel opener gehouden. Als laatste bleek dat al hun boten het water uitgingen voor onderhoud dus ook de toets werden allemaal gecancelled. Toch behoorlijk wat minpunten voor 160 Euro per nacht !

Would not recommend at all! This campsite has what they describe as a “small mouse problem,” which is completely misleading. There are small, easy-to-miss signs, but no clear warning at booking or check-in. Despite following all recommended precautions (and extra advice found online), we were woken in the night by multiple mice running through our campervan.

The site was overrun — mice were everywhere on the ground and throughout the campsite, with everyone we spoke to dealing with multiple mice, not just the odd one. It was impossible to sit or stand outside, and we felt unsafe sleeping in our van. We were forced to spend the rest of the night in the communal bathrooms until sunrise. Unfortunately, these were filthy. Our first visit involved an overflowing toilet covering the floor with no warning or cordon. The kitchen was also unhygienic and not fit to cook in.

We called the out-of-hours number for help and were spoken to in an extremely patronising and dismissive way, being called “darling,” “love,” and “sweetheart,” and told comments like “well look where you are.” Completely inappropriate. Other campsites on our two-week WA road trip did not have this issue. This is clearly a management and hygiene problem, not an unavoidable part of camping.

In the morning, after cleaning droppings from our belongings, we went to reception after being told traps would be available, only to be informed these are for villa guests only, not campsite guests — despite campers being most affected. While the receptionist was polite, it felt like the issue is being actively downplayed.

We spoke to multiple neighbouring campers, all of whom had the same problem, many far worse. Food was ruined, droppings were found on clothes and bedding, and people were shocked the site was even open in this condition.

This campsite is unhygienic, poorly managed, and severely lacking in customer service. At the very least, guests should be clearly warned before arrival — though most would likely cancel.

If you want to be overrun with mice, have your belongings ruined, and be treated as nothing more than dollar signs rather than actual guests — book here.

We stayed in a two bedroom villa for 2 nights. The location is great and the service is superb. As we stayed in  the ground floor, the noice from the upstair guests walking on the wooden floors was little disturbing. Being a small town everything is within a walking distance. The price is on the higher side but understandable due to the remote location. Coral viewing, Manta Ray and  snorkeling tours can be booked at the reception or online which are reasonably priced.
Excellent response to emergency call we made after hours. Thank you

After reading the reviews, I wasn't expecting much, especially after just leaving the beauty Monkey Mia RAC resort. However, after three nights, this has been our favourite place after travelling all the way down to Freo from Port Hedland over a three week period. Beautiful grassy sites for the van, ablutions are more than adequate, great kids spaces, lovely pool, fantastic Reef Cafe with seriously good food and cocktails-open til late with a great vibe, and an impeccable location! The beaches here are insane. Also, only one dead mouse and staff were lovely. We are in a remote spot here, so if you can't deal with some hot salty boar water and regular (not five stars) facilities, then stay in the city✌🏼

Worst campsite experience. We stayed here for just one night, and that was more than enough.

The camp kitchen is absolutely disgusting. Everything is covered in mouse droppings, and the fridge looks and smells like something has died inside. Yes, they mention that they have a “mouse problem,” but this is not just that — it’s a serious hygiene problem. The very least they could do is clean up the mouse droppings.

While having breakfast, we even witnessed their so-called “cleaning process.” A man used a single cleaning cloth that looked like it hadn’t been replaced since World War II. He wiped every surface with it — including the toaster — and was finished in about three minutes. That’s not cleaning at all.

To make things worse, there is an old bird’s nest in the bathroom.

Completely unacceptable. We would never stay here again.

Also quite expensive for what you get.

⭐ 2/5 – Beautiful view, but for the price we expected far more

We stayed at the White House in Coral Bay’s Bayview Resort with a group of seven. The view was truly beautiful and the house itself is spacious, but unfortunately that’s where the positives ended.

The shower leaked and flooded the bathroom every time it was used. Windows and doors rattled constantly, and the floorboards didn’t fit properly, leaving noticeable gaps. The kitchen was poorly equipped — no oven mitts, only one sharp knife, and rubbish bags that didn’t even fit the bin. There was no WiFi, the pillows were flat, the mattresses uncomfortable, and we even heard something living in the walls at night.

For the price we paid, we expected much more care, maintenance, and basic comfort. The location is great, but the property needs serious attention before we’d consider staying again.

Eine der schlechteren Campingplätze auf unserer West Coast Reise. Duschen wirken 50Jahre alt. Es gibt nur Salzwasser in den Bädern und dann noch $65 verlangen ist zu viel. Zudem sind die Sites sehr kurz. Wir hatten 1m zum Nachbarn. Aktuell wird auch gebaut. Von dem Mäuseproblem haben wir nur gelesen aber nichts gesehen.
Coral Bay is a beautiful place to visit for walks swimming and site seeing, and great shopping experience.

If you love paying a lot of money ( the most expensive camp we had to pay on the coast for a unpowered site) and do not sleep a single night correctly because you Van is overrun with little mice, this place is for you !!

If you have any doubts and with a campervan/caravan ect, honestly it’s not worse it.
We were warned at the time of booking that due to the season they had "a small animal problem" and we had to keep the door of the vehicle tightly closed, which we did. Unfortunately when I come to mince, you can close what you want it’s would never been enough. And so now we are the proud owners of a little mouse that has adopted us and our van. Still there even after left since few days.
All this accompanied by a campsite that was barely passable in terms of cleanliness. The bathrooms are really not the best we've seen along the way, and once again, for the most expensive price on the coast.

🐭🚨 Staying here was the biggest mistake of our trip. It was impossible to sleep because the grounds are infested with mice that climb into the car at night. There are hundreds of thousands of them. Our van was damaged because they chewed on the seats and cables. The showers and toilets were not clean either.
Bon camping ⛺️  ,sanitaire propre  mais beaucoup de souris sur le site... faite attention a votre nourriture  ,elle rentre dans les véhicules.

Booked 2 Nigaloo villas via reputable travel agent. Marketed as 'ideal for honeymooners'. Not sure when the blurb was written, but not my idea of 'honeymoon' standard accommodation. Bathroom sink drain smelt badly and everything looked old and tired. As we had booked 3 nights here, decided to pay the excess and upgrade (at a high premium). Swapped to a brand new top floor 3 bedroom apartment which was a vast improvement. However, considering this slept 8, there was only 1 bathroom and no washing machine, yet it had a full size American style fridge freezer. The showers wasn't great, but did the job. It didn't bother us, but the area & hence our accommodation was susceptible to mice. We were provided with a humane trap that worked.
Amenities blocks dirty and old. Bbqs old and dirty as well. Not a nice place in this regard.

Most expensive mistake on our trip so far. $62 for an unpowered site, and they don’t tell you there’s a huge mice problem. I understand there’s a difference between “mouse” and “native marsupial”, but when you have handfuls of them running through your campervan and you can’t sleep because they’re finding nooks and crannies you didn’t even know existed - you take the L and run away. We left before midnight, and can confirm the free camp 40minutes away is better for anyone not living in a completely airtight box.
If you know there’s a problem, either fix it or warn your customers ahead of time so we can make informed decisions.
Couldn’t even walk to the bathroom without running into at least a couple of mice.
Big park with smaller bays. |Bore water so fill your tanks before you arrive and BYO drinking water (unless you want to buy it). |Clean toilets and friendly staff.
Great as always.

Coral Bay ist ein wunderschöner Ort, jedoch lässt die Sauberkeit und die Infrastruktur zu wünschen übrig. Die Zimmer sind sehr ungepflegt, in der Küche sind die Utensilien total verschmutzt. Der Preis ist für einen solchen Standard total zu hoch.
We stayed overnight in the overflow campground & we were grateful it was an option, we wanted to stay & enjoy this beautiful area 👍🤿🌠⛱️🐟🐠🪸we were here in august

This was the start of a short road trip southbound down WA Coral coast and what better way to start than at Coral Bay Hotel. We were sited in a chalet lodge at rear of park, it was perfect; basic, clean and safe accom. We were there for the reef and the snorkelling, 5 min walk to beach, swimming on the reef, across from supermarket and Bills restaurant (equally great). One build is if we knew there were beach front villa’s (looked relatively new) we would have opted for them. Just because they overlooked the beach/bay. Thank you Team Coral Bay Resort.
No frest water on your site. No glass dusty Another thing are good
Wir hatten eine schöne Cabin hier. Der Sanitärbereich war sauber und ausreichend für alle.

Had a quick read of the reviews and just looked up mice under the reviews and 4 months ago it was a issue and it is still an issue mice everywhere even though we don't leave food open I even found them in my ute the mice problem is really a big problem.

The location is amazing, however I was very disappointed with the cleanliness of the amenities. The same dirt remained in the showers throughout our week-long stay, and toilet paper was left on the floors in the toilets without being cleaned up. For such a great location, the standard of cleaning was very disappointing.
It's always a pleasure staying at Ningaloo Bayview.  Close to the beach and all amenities, and Jacqui and the staff are always accommodating.  Would highly recommend staying here.
NO WI-FI. Lovely spacious room with a little kitchenette, however lighting was dim which could really be improved. We had the twin room which had 3 beds so there was a spare for us to put our stuff.
Beautiful spot to stay in caravan nice and peaceful, BUT A bit expensive for an overnight stay when you consider no wifi, no radio, no TV and no fresh water, should be $40 pn, friendly staff.

⚠️🐀🐭🐁⚠️Within hours of arriving at our campsite we discovered several mice scurrying around in our van. It turns out this is a regular and known issue which we weren't made aware of. If we had've been warned upon checkin we could have avoided a completely sleepless night, mouse wee and droppings through our van and damage to property. We were supposed to stay 2 nights but we decided to get out of there. We were told a refund was impossible. Ninagloo Coral Bay, please be upfront about this issue and explain to guests upon arrival what they can do to prevent this happening (ie. Locking away food and keeping windows and doors shut after dark). Don't just expect people to notice your tiny print-out taped to one side of the front desk.
When it comes to judging caravan parks it's all about the showers. And these ones are sub par.
Great caravan park, had powered site no water hookup. Drinking water is available at amenities bore water for showers.
Very well appointed caravan park within walking distance to the beach and small shopping complex.

Turned up at 6:59pm - Reception closes at 7pm. It was dark. It was raining.
As I approached the Reception Glass doors, the young woman looked directly at me and as I motioned to say “Help!”, she locked the door and walked off.  She could have said, “I’m sorry, we are closed now but if you head across the road, they are open for a bit longer”. Instead, she left me - and the two people who turned up minutes later - stranded and worried what we would do for the night.  Appalling behaviour.
The location is amazing so close to so much snorkeling. Some of the staff could try to be happier and work with tourists especially the people who haven’t been there before.

Absolut empfehlenswert. Schöner Pool und Tennisplatz , gratis Mückenschutzspay in den Camp kitchen. Mäuse gibt es keine, geschweige eine Plage.  Preis Leistung viel besser als in anderen CP dort. Die amenity waren alle sauber. Heisses Bohrwasser, jedoch bekommt man ein Mal 20 Liter frisches Wasser gratis dazu .
Absolutely furious. Arrived at closing at 7pm, but instead of advising us we
Too busy and noisy. Amenities are old but clean. Not worth the money you pay.

Convenient stopover with great beach access

Ningaloo Coral Bay Bayview was our ninth stop on a campervan journey from Broome to Perth in July/August 2025. We arrived via sealed roads from Cape Range National Park through Exmouth. Since we had already spent a few days around Exmouth, Coral Bay was planned as a one-night stopover — and that turned out to be a good decision.

Coral Bay is small and charming, with a stunning beach, a few restaurants, and all the essentials like a grocery store and fuel station. The campground itself is quite packed and busy, with little greenery or privacy between sites. However, the facilities were very clean, and the kids loved the large playground with a jumping pillow and plenty of climbing structures.

There are lots of tour options, including diving and snorkeling, but we had already done those in Exmouth. So for us, Coral Bay was perfect for a short stay — one night was just right.
Very touristy can't fish many places there was told you can fish off the boat jetties. But when fishing off them I was told I could not not very good for land based fishing.

Such a disappointment , as a surprise my friend had booked the new villas at Bayview nearest the beach , at $650 a night we expected at least some sort of luxury but these rooms were bare , badly designed and no finishing  touches , even having a furniture cube in the room with only a box of tissues in it and a mop and bucket leant up beside it  was just awful ( we noticed a provided Kmart kettle so why not a few $2 Knick knacks ?) no mirror to view your outfits ( and come on us girls need a mirror ) the bathroom was bare and not exciting in the least , the toilet roll holder is way behind the actual toilet meaning you have to stretch backwards to get toilet roll ( surely the builders know where they should go ) the locked up shampoo , conditioner also behind the spray  of water and hard to reach , the tv on the  side wall has a couch facing the front of the property , so basically only one person can watch tv from the couch in a 6 bed apartment ( my friend had to pull up a dining chair )
The bedrooms were bland with no niceties
All this is such a shame as we were very excited to experience these rooms and had my video camera ready to record the “ beauty “ of the room and share it online , yes the view is lovely but sadly that’s the only things going for these rooms at this price
Would not recommend these rooms at this price unless the finishing touches are completed and  the design flaws fixed
As a general rule we love Bayview and it’s our go to in coral bay so to be disappointed like this was a bit heartbreaking
On returning to our town people asked us what they were like and sadly we had to be honest , wouldn’t want others to spend that amount of money for such a lack of nice touches

Lovely place to visit. Lots of happy campers. I had a powered campsite with water and it was really good. I only gave it 4 stars for the excellent site because it was tricky getting in as the sites are not very deep and cars are parked on the edge of the access roads. Otherwise once you are in it is VERY good. The toilets and showers are great and there is drinking water near the showers and the restaurant. The site water is good for washing but as it is bore water it varies greatly in quality. Always looks clear with varying temperatures. That’s why the owners go to great lengths to supply filtered drinking water which is excellent. Showers use bore water and I must say they were extremely good. Not any complaints there. The whole campground is super clean and tidy. The surrounding areas outside the campground have a  holiday feel. Small shops and cafes are supported by a shopping centre with a nice pub and supermarket plus tour booking shops and medical services. Fuel is available and if you have a boat there is a very good boat ramp a few minutes drive away. The bay and beach are also top rated (by me). I will return!

Don’t do it. Staff are dead behind the eyes and condescending (one lady was amazing). You’ll get a site here because heaps of people leave early. Why you ask? Beacsue water is charged per 10 litres and to extend your stay, you may have to literally drive out at 10 and have to drive back in at 11. Even though the site is empty (never ever experienced this across Australia) To lazy to check and help people out whilst charging $70 a pop. With that hour, please drive to Exmouth and stop giving these guys your money. Once the RAC up the road is built, we may return to the lovely Coral Bay.
Park is within a 10 minute walking distance to everything, $138 for two nights in a small unpowered sight was a bit hefty though!

It’s really shameful that a business which has a monopoly over a location with little other competition decides to charge exorbitant rates for sub-standard, run down & tired accommodation! We spent a couple of nights in different villas & paid nearly $800 for the “privilege”, both villas were in need of upgrading, they appeared to be firmly stuck in the 80’s/90’s era, the first night we were sharing the place with Mice & the second night it was Cockroaches. The bathrooms were particularly poor with corroded shower frames, stained toilet seats & shower bases. We won’t return to Coral Bay & couldn’t recommend this place to others!!
Nice place, great staff - wish I knew no fresh water for my tanks or I’d have filled up on the way.. all water is salty artesian so be aware when you come

There is a mouse problem in this caravan park and when I queried it at reception after having issues the woman I spoke to was quite condescending. Location is great but so is the other caravan park in the area. We chose to leave early rather than dealing with the mice.
Great part of the West Coast, be prepared for it to be super, super touristy though.

We were put on a very sandy powered site that was very difficult to get into. When we set up our 17.5ft poptop the jockey wheel  jockey plate and the board they sat on sunk into the sand swallowing the wheel like quicksand! No way should any caravan be put on site 82!

First up there is no fresh water on your site. You will need a larger tank if you want  to last without having to unhook and fill up or transport water to your van. We have 180l that lasted us fine for the week. We were not notified or told about the water situation and were lucky someone told us prior to us heading out so we made sure our tanks were full.
We were camped in the overflow section above the shops. Our site was large enough for our van and car however the road access is very tight and we were lucky no one was opposite us when we arrived which made it ok to get on our site.
The amenities in our area are demantable types and were kept clean and stocked every day.
We were quite close to the shops and pub and could even hear the musicians each night which was nice.
there is a washing machine but no drier and it‘s only a cold wash (warm is not aloud) … weird for this price

Just a short walk over the dunes to the stunning beach. Marine life fir all to see with great snorkelling on the nigaloo reef in the bay. Fins Cafe has great coffee and a good selection of dinner choices.
Camping ground sites adequate size, facilities plentiful & well located. Opposite shopping, cafe & beach.
A fantastic stay during school holidays. A group of 21 adults and kids...all placed together on 6 lots... close to everything. Couldn't have asked for more. Can't wait to return.

We booked a Bay view Unit, 2 bedroom. It was small, nice enough, but in direct view of the street both in the living area and the balcony. But from my point of view, I know it is in a beach side tourist town, but if you are charging 650 per night for a modest one story villa, you have to install a washing machine and provide wifi. Management says that 'Telstra reception is not strong enough' but that is absurd, any place of this size can install Starlink now. I could understand if this was a budget option but at this price, backing into a caravan park.....
Ok caravan Park,  located in a great spot, but staff not too friendly. Expensive and no water
Got the waterfront villa. They are greatly equipped and super well located. Only wifi is missing.

Motorhome.  Great site at the Bayview C/Park.  No on site water which was a bit of a surprise however you could fill up with water cheaply.  Beautiful ocean, great showers, fully stocked little supermarket with everything you could need, perfect bakery and eateries.  Coral Bay for me was perfect.  I wished it was closer to South Australia.  Book early if you don't want to miss out. Especially during school holidays.

Quite disappointing. For the rates they charge per night for a room which is AUD 310 for the Ningaloo Villa, the property was quite run down. To my amazement, there is no wifi in the accommodation. This is the only place I stayed in my life so far that doesn’t offer wifi to its guests.
I know people come to such places to do sea sports but at night when there is nothing to do, surfing the internet becomes an important past time.

Most difficult to access caravan site ever . Cars were parked all along the narrow roads . Would have been impossible to get into site had 3 other cars not moved for us . Unfortunately there in school holidays and it was packed and noisy

Great location. Some staff were great, a couple of dead behind the eyes backpackers though. Tiny sites, but expected in school holidays. They cant do much about being popular, and some of the thoughtless clientele can ruin the ambience.

Sharing what is supplied in a GARDEN VILLA  kitchen because this would’ve helped me pack and there is limited info online.
5x dinner plates, bowls, side plates
5x forks, knives, spoons, teaspoons
1x tongs, wooden spoon, egg flip, masher, lge serving spoon, can opener.
1x bread and 1x cutting knife (very basic and not overly sharp - pretty standard)
1x colander
2x saucepans with lid
1x salad bowl
1x grater
1x microwave bowl
1x water jug
1x breadboard
5x wine glasses
5x water glasses
5x mugs
1x toaster
1x kettle
1x iron, ironing board, mop, bucket, broom and dustpan/brush.
1x hairdryer
Polyester bed linen is very noisy and not comfortable to sleep on so consider BYO
Pillows are old and uncomfortable.
Extra blanket per bed is supplied.

If your thinking of staying at this caravan park, stop and think twice.|We booked 9 months ago and found ourselves in the overflow section with minimal amenities 300m walk to the Cafe and pool.  The attitude of the manager when asked to please explain was appahling I think he needs a permanent holiday.|Here are a few photos.

Think twice b4 staying at this CP.  Firstly, the manager is extremely rude, and secondly, we were put in the rear overflow section despite booking 9 months in advance.  When questioned why we were put in that location, we were not given a satisfactory explanation. This overflow is approx 300m away from the cafe, pool, kids area, etc... and of course you pay the same amount.  The amenities are 3 star minimum. 1 star for customer service.

Worst place we’ve stayed while travelling Western Australia, service is appalling, amenities are filthy with 6 inches of grotty water on the floor, laundry machines are so clogged up with muck they can’t accept coins, extremely overpriced and overrated

29/6/2026
Staying in the camp ground.

Head  bang terrible load music till well after 9.pm From Bills bar on site.
Do not stay here if you want a peaceful night.

Coral Bay is a great location, this park and the check in staff were a let down for us. We received notification of a mice issue before arriving however upon arriving were told the site we payed for with power and water only had a salt water hookup.. For us this is much more important than a couple of mice. We were referred to the booking email where they had 'stated this'. Upon reviewing our booking confirmation,  there is no mention of no fresh water. It does mention it on the website we later found. This can be purchased when leaving for 10 cents per litre from the caravan park or 20 cents from the servo. There is also a drinking water only communal tap.
1 star for the young staff members at the counter and 2 stars for the park for not having fresh water. 5 stars for the location..

Wouldn’t stay here again and wouldn’t recommend it! They have a big mouse problem. Unfortunately, we realised it way too late, and they kept us awake the whole night. The staff was a bit rude and not helpful at all, so we had a really unpleasant stay/experience.

$62 for a few square meters of space a night is a rip of, seems like they’re taking advantage of the location. I wonder also if they properly treat Bore water in showers? not safe for skin and hairs !

We booked in for 2 nights and were warned at check-in about a small mice issue. We didn’t think much of it at first. That evening, while sitting at the lovely bar in the park, we saw three mice running underneath our feet. We laughed it off, assuming it was just part of the outback experience.

But that night in our campervan was a different story. We heard mice in at least five different locations around the van, scratching, chewing, and clearly trying to get in. It was constant and impossible to ignore. When we eventually heard one inside the van, we packed up and moved to the Coral Bay carpark at 2am just to get some peace.

We cancelled our second night and were refunded for one night, but the receptionist seriously downplayed how bad the issue was. This wasn’t a minor inconvenience – it was a full-blown infestation.

Coral Bay is a beautiful town and we loved the area, but the park needs to take this problem more seriously. We’re a young couple and usually laid-back, but this was beyond ridiculous.
Very very very good. Right in the heart of it.
Convenient location for Bill's Bay , facilities reasonably well maintained, staff in the front desk very helpful.  Little bit tight with 30ft van when busy ,but doable

Pretty cool place, right next to the beach, easy access to tours and had a funky café near the front with rock lobster and crocodile.

Was expensive and we couldn't use the water pump for the caravan as it was contaminated with salt water but there were drinking stations provided, a 1 dollar fee to fill up caravan tank per litre.

Sites were huge which was a great plus.

This site is in the perfect location however riddled with a mouse infestation. We were told when checking in there had been some mice seen on site recently, however we didn’t realise the extent of the problem. When the sun went down they were everywhere you looked and we are now completing our roadtrip up north with mouse traps in the van. We even kept all food in the shared kitchen and kept doors closed as much as possible but this didn’t deter them. Otherwise the facilities were clean and lots of amenities to use.

Amazing camping site! Maintenance and cleaning service 👌. The check-in process is simple, and the girls are very welcoming, helpful, and friendly! The bathrooms need a renovation, but the shower is wonderful!

Convenient location to Bills Bay and Foodworks. However amenities were subpar including bore water through most taps including the showers and a lack of hot water. There were lots of bugs and mice around the campsite and be very mindful of if you are located under a tree as there will be lots of clean up from the birds.

Fuimos al camping en un powered site. Amplio lugar, con pasto y regadores, las calles con adoquines y cemento. El complejo es hermoso, súper bien situado y tiene cocina, baños con agua caliente, bares, restaurante, cafés, panadería, algunos locales para compras, supermercado y heladería artesanal, locales de compra de equipos de diving y más. El lugar tiene una vibra muy linda, la gente demasiado amable y muy tranquila. La gente de recepción es divina, te atienden super bien y dan buenas recomendaciones para aprovechar al máximo la estadía. Volvería sin dudas💯

Arrived at Bayview Coral Bay to be told that they do not have water, only bore water. Well that would have been great to know before we arrived. I was told that it has been that way for the past 80 years and well that’s great when you actually come from NSW and have no idea. We were told we could fill up around at a tap which costs money so we pulled up in a MH that we weren’t quite sure whether we would be able to turn around due to the cars parked in the area. So I go to the office and ask for the key and to pay $20 bond and then told water is turned off at the moment due to construction and should be on at 11am. It was 10.58am  so we sat at the MH and I checked in every 30 mins just to see if it was in yet and was looked at if I was a pain in the arse. We had been travelling for hours and just wanted to set up and stop. In the end we just set up and thought we would just have to pack up again and fill up when it came back on. The park also had a lot of people sitting up drinking until 2am in the morning. The people behind us ended up having a domestic until 3am in the morning. Obviously not enough security checking up on the 10pm curfew. Many people in our street were upset about putting up with this and couldn’t wait to move on. Then today I go to the fish feeding and the young lady with dark hair and bob from the office that I was dealing with was extremely rude to me. All I was doing was going under the water to film the fish as others were doing. Very disappointed in the park and will not be recommending it to others.

$70 for a crappy campsite, everyone crammed in to make the most profit. The toilets and showers are dirty, the kitchen is also dirty and nothing is working  inside. Of course no WIFI.
One of the worst camping for this price.

My best friend and I stayed in the Balcony Room at Bay Lodge and we had a great time. The room was clean, comfortable and well-equipped, with everything we needed to look after ourselves during the stay. It’s the kind of place where you maintain your own space, which suited us perfectly.

The location is a big plus — it’s close to everything, including the beach, cafes, and tour pick-up points. We especially loved having a balcony to relax on after a day out exploring.

Overall, it was a good experience, and we’d happily stay here again.

Over all it was good, but we discovered 3 mice in our 3 bed apartment...I was given a mouse trap by reception. I caught all the mice, Unfortunately one mouse did lots of poos in my bed. I wasn't offered any thing, an offer to move to a mouse free villa would be nice or 100 off as I had a very sleepless night. I'm not sure I would stay again unless they put flyscreens on the front doors as our unit was always full of bugs, some minor updates are really needed in the old villas, it's a bit tired, the sheets and bedding are also very old.

Coral Bay is one big accommodation village on a beautiful bay with clear water.|We booked a room in the Bayview lodge for 3 nights. The room was clean, kitchen with utensils, nice size bath room and comfortable beds. Location wise also good.|Like almost anywhere in Western Australia you can hear everything from your room and bad luck on the 2nd night with noisy neighbors and slamming doors. We had two relaxing days though but if you have a choice the Bay villa’s looked new and better. Overall a good stay and friendly staff at reception and restaurants. Take your own food if possible since the supermarket is expensive. The bottle shop and restaurants are decently priced and enough choice. Note - RAC is constructing another property, did not bother us but it gets bigger and busier…….in the end 2 nights would have been enough for us.
Pricey but Coral Bay is amazing.

We have stayed at bayview a few times
Now. Firstly, its located beautifully in town. 2 min walk from everything. The grounds are also kept as beautiful as possible with hundreds of people coming in and out, im impressed they can keep the grass so nice. Unfortunately, it is let down by the bathrooms. They are terribly run down, they are slippery 24/7 which with little ones is a nightmare. The bathrooms are unbearably hot in the evenings with no ventilation. And they are not very clean. I noticed the drains had the same clumps of hair in them our entire stay. Plus dead bugs all over! My second issue was smoking. We were surrounded by people smoking im every direction. We were very close to the children's playground, and the camp kitchen and all the time we were inhaling smoke. It should be a smoke free area! Was very disappointed with this. We are unsure if we would stay again.
Great park and facilities across the road from beach and shops

Expecting this place to be a highlight of our lap,  and hearing it gets heavily booked, we booked almost 12 months in advance. I had heard that people are travelling with dogs often get relegated to the top paddock behind Bills Bar and the Backpackers, so requested a site in the main area closer to better facilities. They do allow dogs down there, so why, booking so early were we not put in the main park?  What a nightmare this caravan park is. I think the differing in ratings depends where you are. We were put up in the top paddock with other ‘Dog people’. The facilities were DISGUSTING.  The worst we’ve seen on our 8 months on the road. The shower heads are so corroded that the water sprays out like needles in all directions and they stink. Cubicles are mouldy, hairy and some have holes in the walls.  They are not cleaned at all.  I stared at the same used tampon wrappers for 3 days despite them being ‘cleaned’ daily. There were mice in there and lots of droppings. The camp kitchen is filthy, with mouse poo everywhere. It is the only place we have stayed where the BBQs were not used once…old dirty iron plates with an inch of mouse poo around them. There is one rusted out washing machine that does not say it is bore water…our neighbours used it and ruined their children’s clothes. It is not sign posted as bore water, yet the machines in the other section have fresh water options.
We had read reviews about a mouse problem and not being too put off, we spent $100 on plastic containers before arriving and put out peppermint oil and dryer sheets to deter them coming in our new motorhome. it is impossible to block off all access holes, but we did what we could. Despite this, we had mice come in every night and poo all over the sink and stove. When we complained, they lent us some humane catch and release traps, which we set each night and managed to catch one a night for the rest of the stay. At least this stopped the mess everywhere. The ground staff, who were lovely said it is just a bad year, but no one likes baits used so they just do absolutely nothing. Whilst I am apposed to baits, you do see other CPs with contained bait stations, just letting the problem get to this level is unacceptable.
Down the hill in the other section, the roads are paved so not as dusty,  the facilities are better , bbq’s newer, there is a pool and as stated the washing machines have fresh water. Why are people charged the same to be up the top, with such disgusting facilities? And they charge $2 per dog just to top it off!  How is this justified.
Coral Bay is just beautiful, we spent a lot of money in the 3 restaurants, snorkelling equipment, food and drink fromthe Foodworks, bottle shop etc..  We really tried to make the most of it but the Caravan Park did spoil our week. Management and owners need to know just how appalling it was. The people camping with their dogs up the top were so friendly and nice but many people left early due to the showers, kitchen and mice. Shame.
A very clean and tidy carvan park plenty of hot showers and good laundry  facilities which made our weekstay very pleasant.

Don't book at this location if you're looking to have a good time... staff don't know the rules of camping here ... and people with kids will ruin your time as no one can play music after the precious little buggers go to bed. 10pm is stated as curfew and was told at 7pm to turn my music down..... would probably just skip this place and camp down the road next time....

Very disappointed with our stay   We have been travelling for 1 month out of a 3 month road trip and this wins the worst. From arriving unfriendly reception terrible site run down ammenities poor shower. The toilet shower block needs to be upgraded. For the most expensive site booking we were not happy. Thank goodness for the natural beauty of the beach. This caravan park could do well to put back into the facility and not just take it in.
Facilities are poorly cleaned and maintained, floors are covered with sand and dirt very unpleasant to go for a shower

Nice roomy sites, green grass, has dump point, drinking water available, a fee charged but we were $3 for 50litres. Note though we use a bore water sediment filter that turned very brown with this fill. Laundry and bathroom functional but a bit tired. 100% would stay here again it was very pleasant.
Staff were friendly and helpful. The park is close to everything available in Coral Bay.

Outstanding customer service regarding our booking changes. Victoire in the Reservation Office provided very prompt, excellent communication with us. |A pleasure to deal with. |Highly recommend. Thank you Ningaloo Coral Bay. We appreciate your understanding and willingness to accommodate our itinerary changes.
Stayed in a triplex family cabin as it was all that was available. Double bed was too small for 2 people, otherwise very happy with the cleanliness and facilities of the cabin and park.

Don’t stay here unless you want your things mice infested. I have never been to a campsite and had this issue before (been to a lot over the years). It was that bad we had to leave despite having a longer stay booked (we cut our loses with the money as didn’t want to stay a minute longer having been up all night worrying and stressing). You aren’t advised at reception when you arrive that there’s an issue. We ended up having to pay a lot of money to remove them from our van (that’s after only staying one night despite our doors closed at all times). So the place is swarming with them. I let reception know about this and she said that they are fully aware about the issue and from what was being said little effort is being done to resolve this. It’s a shame as the location is lovely. So don’t stay here unless you want a vehicle/ tent full of mice that you can’t get rid of. Very disappointed worst experience I have ever had on a campsite.

This park is very overpriced compared to many great well kept parks in great locations around Australia.
Check in was easy but staff provided no information on the area - had to find out for ourselves.
Busy park and if you have a dog  you are placed down the back. Rules not really applied as there were dogs off leads, walking around  in enclosures,  constant barking etc
Pool is nice but smallish and many kids were in it (not school holidays)
Ablution blocks need an upgrade - quite a lot of grime around edges, peeling paint  etc
As drinking water is not in ready supply take with you and buy well before Exmouth - from $4 in Carnarvon to $14 for 10 L in Coral Bay.
Close to main beach, supermarket, bakery, hotel , cafes etc
If you take your pooch be aware that there are many restrictions in the area about where they can go

We stayed in one of their two overflow campsites. Even if the campsite is booked out, call to check if the overflow is available its $62 a night, safe sites with hot shower and toilet block. Good option if you're doing a whaleshark trip out of Coral Bay.

I stayed in the family cabin. The air conditioner worked well.

At night there was an animal scratching in the roof. An info sheet said there might be native mice around.
Are there any comments on the mice plague  there

Campingplass. Feiret julaften her samme med familien. Kjekt å se hvordan de «lokale»  feirer jul. Plassen er fin. Kjekt å ha gressplen  så en slipper all sanda inn i campingbilen. Fasiliteten bra, men kjøkkenet blir raskt fullt/kø. Lag mat tidlig - eller stikk over gata på pub’en og få deg mat.

Six of us stayed in a 3 bedroom villa on the top floor with a fantastic view of the sea. Apartment was clean and provided all the basics. We had a small issue with the hot/cold water but the staff were really helpful and managed to fix it for us. If I ever make it back to Ningaloo, I’d definitely stay here again. The apartments are relatively new and I especially liked the photographs they’ve hung up. They also provided a helpful guide of activities, tours, restaurants etc.
We also had issues with the mices coming in our tent and in the car. Avoid the unpowered sites as the mices live in the bush at the back of the site.

‼️Beware of mice problem. We were not notified of this issue prior to our arrival and had our car infested with mice, with a 10 month old this was appalling to me. Her stuff was covered in poo and bottle teats chewed. I spoke to reception and was told they were aware, quite disappointed that we were not notified of this issue! Left a sour taste in our mouth. We come every year and have never had this issue. Apparently it’s just nature 🤮 beyond mortified. We stayed in site 191.

We needed a last minute unpowered site on Easter Sunday as we couldn’t get to the campsite we had booked, Bayview had an overflow at $60 a night, which was quite expensive, but there is warm showers and clean toilets, access to the pool and the pitch was pretty big, overall recommend if you need a last minute site!

This place was nice but busy! Wasn’t even school holidays yet. Bays and roads are a bit tight for bigger caravans but you’ll get there. Nice pool and playground for the kids. Easy walking across the street for the little mall and down the street for the beach. You can’t use the site water for drinking. Comes straight from an artesian bore, hot and salty.

Absolutely disgusting. Tried out both the 1 bedroom cabin and the family cabins. Just gross. I have stayed in a range of caravan parks and this is absolutely appalling. Stained sheets, day/s old cleaning cards with different peoples names on them, spare blanket was covered in dust and white stains, both rooms had an absolutely rancid smell. Just gross!!!

We stayed on the campsite (in a tent, unpowered) and enjoyed our 3 nights stay! Staff is very friendly and helpful, campsite is quiet after 9.30/10 which is always appreciated and there are some good facilities like a communal kitchen (will need to bring your own pots/pans etc), fridge, freezer. The Reef bar (next to the reception) has free wifi for customers. Very close to the beach and small shopping arcade, you can walk everywhere.

Toilets/showers were clean. Be aware: you shower with the bore water (which is slightly salty), but I didn’t mind! There are free fresh water taps throughout the campsite to fill your tanks/bottles.

Only downside: we paid $62 per night for 2 adults which I think is a bit steep for an unpowered spot.
Beautiful 😍

The location is amazing and people are very kind, we had a good snorkeling tour.
The rooms for the price are really run down, especially bathrooms in the private villas are below any expectations!!! Even public toilets are better maintained.
This took away a lot from the otherwise good experience.

BE WARY!! This company took a 50% deposit from my credit card when I booked through Booking.com even though there was nothing on their listing of the room saying that they would do this.  My booking was for 6 months from the booking date.  I was not happy about this and promptly cancelled my booking asking for the full refund they had stated on the room booking was part of the conditions as long as it was up to 30 days before the trip.  They refunded most but not all the money stating that they were withholding money to cover the credit card transaction.  They have refused to repay the full amount even though the booking clearly stated they would do this.
DO NOT TRUST THEM WITH YOUR HARD EARNED MONEY.  They quite simply do not do what they say they will.

Don’t book a Bayview room if you want peace and quiet. The park across the street is used by backpackers to party all night. Paying $500 a night to listen to doof doof when we came for peace and quiet. You won’t get upscale customers with backpackers around. Exmouth is better.

We stayed in the one bedroom cabin with no bathroom (use amenities block across the road) - and it was just perfect for us! Clean, well maintained, kitchenette seemed renovated or pretty new. Only downside was the aircon wasn't quite strong enough for the summer 42 degree heat in March - but the staff quickly provided a pedestal fan when we asked for one - thank you! Highly recommend this budget option

backpackers who just don't care. I waited over 20mins to speak with the tour bookings person who actively ignored me the whole time I was waiting at the counter, in the end I walked out. |definitely think about whether you really want to visit the area.

Very good campsite. 1 minute walk to the beach, different types of accommodation, powered sites, unpowered sites and rooms as well.
Pool available, it is small and unless you have kids I would skip it to just go to the beach.
Family friendly with kids playground.
Different bathrooms throughout the camp with potable water station. The bathrooms were clean.
The grass is cured and a restaurant opening at night can cure your hunger.
Best thing of all is the bay, the place itself it's beautiful and this js the campsite you want to be in.
Clean, safe and green.
Until we meet again.
Quiet this time of year but staff are welcoming and informative, amenities clean and park very neat

Fabulous place to visit. We came in February which is the hottest month but this made it very quiet. The site offers camping spots, cabins, and slightly bigger accommodation. The showers etc were all clean and the site is well looked after. When we first arrived it turned out we had booked a cabin without bathroom facilities.Luckily there was one available that did so we paid extra to switch to that one. All the fantastic dive/ snorkel trips are run here so you can book those too. Definitely something not to miss as they are very well run and you see so much . All equipment is provided too. The staff are very friendly and helpful I would highly recommend it

Absolute rip off! Wayyy too overpriced for what little you get! Showers and toilets are dirty. I was able to have a hot shower using only the cold tap. And sites have no shade. Save yourself the disappointment from the money hungry site and carry on up to Exmouth
Great place to stay 👌

We stayed 3x nights in unit at top..can see the ocean but new 2x story units have blocked most of the view.
The beds are horrible like sleeping on concrete.
Only good thing was close to beach.

We loved our stay in Coral bay. What an amazing place.
Camp site were good but shower and toilet weren’t clean and smelly.
We went to the restaurant and the seafood platter was amazing!
Lovely location and super views

Just stayed 8 nights over two sights at a cost of around $1000. The park is EMPTY. Asked for a later check out of around 12 noon and were promptly told ‘absolutely no late check outs under any circumstances’. Even though the day earlier we were told to check back at reception the day before leaving.

Stay here for sure… just don’t expect any leeway or service at all. And make sure you’re out at 10 on the dot, or you’ll have the staff hassling you in golf carts… we’ve seen it since we’ve been here even though as we said…. The park is EMPTY.
Average campsite. I think it would be squashed in here in the summer !

Great location and as it's low season there are not many people here so lots of space. We were allocated a big site but can imagine it would be challenging in high season on many of the sites. Lovely pool area. Quick walk to the stunning beaches here. Took our dog to Paradise beach every day he loved it.
Great place to rest up

Stayed only one night but will definitely be back for a longer stay
Perfect position to walk to the most gorgeous beach and Bill's Pub for a reat meal.

We had a Bayview Unit high at the rear which had great view of the beach and ocean.   It was well maintained, had all the amenities we needed and was just a short walk to the beach.   Access is via the back stairs, so you need to be mobile.  Would definitely stay there again.

There is only 2 caravan parks in this area and street.  This one is the only dog friendly park.  Both across from a non-dog friendly beach.  The dog friendly beach is over the sand dune at the end of the street.
Park is average.  Nice to have some grass.  Facilities average, but ok.
Extremely overpriced! Paid more than in Broome and got a lot more there.  Paid a lot for a powered site.  But you cannot use water as not treated.  They have a water bulk fill for caravans.  They make you pay $20 security for key to use (fair) but then only let you get 20 litres!!!!!! Charge you a $1  a litre after that!!!! Rip off!!!! When you have stayed several nights and used your own water in that high price.  The least they could do is let you fill your van back up.
There is also no discount for solo travellers or seniors.
It is all about the location for this park.
Having a dog made it a necessity.
Was a disappointing end.

Very bad problem solving, I was on an UNPOWERED site for “Only” $55 and the guys in front of us were shouting screaming and music was on until 1am
We tried to call after hour  but no answer
They said there was a night guard  but we saw nobody.
The Day after we spoke to reception and management and they said “all I can do is to apologize, no refunds.”
I suggest you guys book somewhere else if you are a fan of kindness and quiet places.
I know it’s not their fault if customers are loud, but certainly they could have handled that differently.

55$ not to sleep is not worth it.
Der Campingplatz liegt ideal nur 5 Minuten vom Meer, Sanitäranlagen sauber und gepflegt, sehr freundlicher Empfang
Good location but when we had noisy french campers on Saturday night, the after hours number was not manned. Dissatisfied.

Schöner Campingplatz an super cooler Lage. Die sanitarischen Einrichtungen sind für australische Verhältnisse gut. Sauberer und neuer als vieles was wir anderweitig in Australien schon erlebt haben.

Okay das Salzwasser zum duschen ist speziell. Mir hat es gepasst. Preis Leistung sicher am oberen Ende aber hey, mit der Aussicht direkt vor der Tür - wer kann es ihnen verübeln. Wir kommen wieder.

We (retired couple) stayed for 3 nights in a Villa. It was clean and quite spacious. Effective air conditioning. Enough equipment to cook basic meals, though there was no oven. At $285/night, I think that we should be given more than one towel each (we queried this, and it's their policy) and a total of three clothes hangers! The complex was fairly quiet. Most of the front office staff were helpful, good-humoured and friendly. The one hour coral-watching trip was pretty lame. Coral Bay is lovely, though in danger of becoming over-developed.

Friendly, helpful staff
Don't miss the shark nursery for lots of rays and 100+ baby sharks - well worth the 20/30 minutes walk
Bayview is a massive mixed use site (camping, rooms etc)

Had a week in the caravan park and loved every minute. And to those reviewers whinging about mains water - get over yourselves. Coral Bay has NO WATER, despite being on the ocean it’s extremely arid and receives very little rainfall. What do you dummies expect? The Trevi Fountain ffs!! The park has drinking water taps which anyone can use to fill containers, otherwise it’s bore water in the taps. Go and stay somewhere else next time idiots.
This place needed a good deep clean

Most expensive site we have ever paid for considering no water sandy site just like the showers.  Come on you owners your making a killing. Time to put some effort back into your park. On-site restaurant gave us dirty glasses sticky old tables and chairs.  Understand it's location.
Coral Bay is one of best locations we have been to.  Ningaloo reef is so interesting and beautiful.

Pre booked 4 night stay, September, powered site. 2 adults. After an incorrect powered tent site allocation, we were redirected to site 130, opposite the amenities and unfortunately also the dump point. The latter made the area very busy and often a bit smelly.
The drinking water is available at points around the park, fill a container for use, water on site is from the bore which seems to be mainly used for watering the grass.
Amenities were partially refurbished, toilets were fine, showers were not, nozzle straight from the wall and a stainless steel trough to stand in, everywhere ends up wet, no where to put toiletries and little hanging space. Mould and peeling paint was evident on the walls. The  block further away was tiled but still had poor drainage. Given the cost of sites and the volume of guests, the amenities need to be so much better.
Easy access to the beaches, shops, cafe and tour shops, quiet park, with small to medium sites near 130.
Staff at reception were helpful and courteous.

We love staying here when we're in Coral Bay. The rooms are clean and comfortable. Until this year, there had always been a gorgeous view of the Bay. Now you see the newly constructed two storey accommodation instead.

So awesome, caravan park is really good. Pool area is also great, well set up for kids having the playground and amenities close together. Staff were all very friendly and the ablutions and showers were all well kept.
Wow, green grass,  green trees, very tidy caravan park, very close to the beach, beach safe environment for toddlers and not so toddlers(adults)

We booked in advanced, and were put over the road. Saying over the road is an understatement, it's in a completely different area so the kids can not just go to the park, 8 min walk to any facility for the kids and still charged full price for them, not happy at all.
We have been travelling for over 5 months and have never had an issue with any other parks, absolute rubbish set up should of been told this at booking. So heads up if you have kids and your booking for a week, make sure you ask where your site will be otherwise it could be a horrible stay.

Fantastic location the caravan park had everything you needed to enjoy your stay
Coral bay was a hot spot for tourists but it was extremely enjoyable
The reef caffe was wonderful as were the staff it was just out the front of the park
Snorkel and swimming just a short walk
Recommend to book in advance as it seems busy in all the caravan parks there
But make the effort as the memory’s will last forever
A lovely, busy caravan park.  Friendly and helpful staff.  Great vibe in the park amongst the campers.

WARNING this caravan park would have to be the worst one we stayed at during our holiday and not a good experience will never return.   No mains water at all and if you want filtered water you need to pay extra on top of the expensive price of the site.  If we could get our money back we would have left and kept on driving.  We got more at the roadhouse stops we stayed. We were not told at the time of booking that we would not get mains water and therefore hadn't planned for this had we known before driving all the way here, we would have made alternate arrangements. The showers were dirty and disgusting in need of updating and also used by the hostel people.  The sites are very small and hard to get into.  The roadways for the cars are very tight.
We would not recommend this place and would never return here.  We had previously  stayed at the People Caravan up the road and that was lovely and they provided water and free filtered water with codes for toilets/showers so that local backpackers don't use them.  What an absolute joke this place is extremely disappointing andvery unhygienic. The sanitary bins have not been emptied in ages they are over flowing and stinks.  If there was a place I could lodge a formal complaint I would.

What a run-down, poorly maintained caravan park. 1 star at most! No money or care has been put back into the facilities of this park, its embarrassing seeing how much revenue they would be making.

Second hand bbqs from mines bought off Sodexo supplied for "campers kitchen" 1 stove top and 1 microwave for the entire camp which aren't clean. Showers and toilets again, run down and dirty. The playground hasn't been maintained or upgraded in what looks like 20+ years.

The poor camp sites next to the toilets smell like sewage, it's disgusting.

Come on Bay View you have the monopoly and the money! Put some work and love back into the park as it deserves and what good customers are paying for! It's an embarrassment to WA tourism!

👍Lovely staff, handy to beach, reasonable amenities. Mostly quiet after 9pm.
👎Yet another overcrowded park, just like Cable Beach, with jammed in sites and vehicles spilling onto drives. Excessively bright  amenities lighting overnight.

After reading the negative reviews we were apprehensive. Yes, the showers/toilets are 'tired', but they get a GOOD clean every morning. I had to go to the toilet at 6am, the guy was there doing a damn good thorough job cleaning them. Would definitely stay here again, and recommend it to others. No noise after 10pm. Great stay.
Very nice, really lived up to all expectations.

Nice beach but the campground is totally overpriced and dirty. Dead animals in the bathroom and a mouse jumped out under the stove when we lifted up the cover.

Do not recommend staying here.

Very average considering how much it costs to stay here
Sites are tiny, no water and the shower blocks are disgusting.
Fair enough, people are gross sometimes but there is mold everywhere. The showers look exactly the same as before they were ‘cleaned’
I get it’s busy and the showers are being used constantly but you can definitely afford to pay people to clean them properly

I would go as far as putting Coral bay on my never to return list.
A pretty place but sadly over the top expensive for accommodation and caravan parks.

Caravan site was encroached by another van. This person had a huge dog pen. There were dogs on 3 sites next us also.
We had to smell dogs wee all the time. It STUNK.This also attracted flies. Very unpleasant stay here.

We loved our few days in Coral Bay and really enjoyed this campsite! The showers were hot with excellent pressure. The kitchen amenities were clean and accessible from all sites. Despite it being the busiest time of year, you never felt too crammed with your neighbours and it was very nice to be able to fill up your water bladders.

Had 2 nights unpowered caravan site here July 20th. Great location opposite Bills bar and the bay. Facilities ok but fixed head showers are too low!!! Fortunately im good at bending over backwards for the wife, so could get my head under the shower no problem 🤣. Sites are small but ok for a couple of nights.

We stayed for a week in a garden villa.
The room was very basic, salty water shower and sinks. The kitchen has a separate drinking water tap that was really good. We found the beds pretty hard however all bed linen and pillows were supplied.

I had to ask at reception for replacement supplies that didn't get replaced for the entire week such as toilet paper, bin bags, shampoo, conditioner soap and dishwashing liquid and no additional service to the room. They changed our towels and that was it.

We didn't mind the lack of room service however if you pay high prices you kind of expect a higher service. We also wanted to use the oven however there were no trays or basic utensils for oven use.

All in all the stay was fun, relaxing and comfortable and kids had a fantastic time. The reception staff were awesome and really helpful, the fish and chips at Bayview is awesome and recommend it. The Mahi-mahi fishing charter i highly recommend (great bunch of guys).
Stayed here June '24 and loved every minute of it. Comfortable rooms, lots of options food-wise, and incredibly helpful staff

My husband and I really enjoyed our 2 nights here and wished it could have been longer.
We were fortunate enough to be allocated a caravan site at the back of the park which allowed us more room and an easier parking of our 20 ft van and car.
The amenities block was kept as clean as possible considering the park was at capacity.
The showers are bore water (which we were fore warned about) however the water was consistently hot with very good pressure.
This caravan park has such a lovely holiday vibe and the location couldn't be better, an easy walk to what must be one of the most beautiful, family friendly beaches in WA.
Also the staff at reception couldn't have been more helpful at fitting us in at short notice and organising tours for us.
We would highly recommend.

Thoroughly enjoyed our 2 week stay at Coral Bay. The Ningaloo caravan park was clean and tidy. Do not connect your van to the water. It is very mineralised bore water. There is drinking water available. Walk to the shops, beach and pub. Street is only 600m long so you don't need to walk far. CPark is dog friendly.
A huge jumping pillow and playground for the kids.
Over priced move on to a better place village is small an crowded

Warning: very small caravan sites! Watched three different people in larger caravans really struggle to fit on their site. The water is quite salty, particularly in the showers. The washing machines were very rusty and looked like they should have been replaced a few years ago. The bathrooms are also in desperate need of repair and update. The worst part was the noise, the bar in the middle of town next to the backpackers plays loud music every night until late and you can clearly hear it inside your van when trying to sleep.

I also watched many other travellers who were clearly just passing through and not staying at the park, park in the car park behind food works and come over to use the showers and camp kitchen. It’s apparently a well known spot to sneak into for free campers to use the amenities during the day. This was annoying because there were only three showers and toilets so always a queue. We were given a site away from the main park, around town behind the car park.

We arrived and checked in and when we got to our cabin there was a backpackers van blocking our entry to unload our stuff as it was raining.
Then in the evening I sent my boys to have a shower and I would meet them there, one of my children said there is a condom in the cubicle to my surprise there was. And in the other cubicle there was faeces all over the doors.
I complained to the reception and they said they would clean it up asap.
Then later just before going asleep took my children to the toilet and one of the toilets was blocked ip with all used toilet paper stuck to the side of the cubicle.
Then I thought i would check the cubicle to see if condom was removed and to my shock it was still there.
Then when me and my wife was getting into bed we notice that the comfort blanket was dirty and smelt like it hadn't been cleaned at all.

Now bearing in mind we paid $180 for 1 night this is unacceptable and disgusting knowing it's a busy period they should have the toilets checked frequently and when a customer tells you something you make sure it's cleaned and removed.

Salt water for showers and taps! Grossly cramped, dirty bathrooms, overall uninterest to help staff. And $55 per night for a small unpowered site! Find a small independent camp-site and jump in the ocean. You will have basically the same facilities then. We checked out after 1 night and went somewhere else.
We thought $55 per night for the overflow camping area is a bit expensive for what it is.

WARNING! We stayed in the Garden View Villa and it was swarming with cockroaches. Every time I went into the main room I found 3-5 more running around. Upon arrival they told us if we wanted to leave early we wouldn't get a refund. Now I know why. I barely slept in fear they would crawl all over me. I almost slept in my car. Take care of your pests! Yuck!!!
Poorly kept premises. water is an additional cost, no Wifi, expensive washing machines. Bore water as campsites only. disappointing given the nice location.

We had a lovely stay in a garden room (room 40). Fully equipped kitchen, clean bathroom, 1 double  bed and another room with 3 single beds. It was spotless when we arrived. Easy walk across the road to the beach. 100 walk to shops and restaurants. Our top tip is to bring food with you. As we were travelling with only carry on we borrowed empty suitcases in Perth- filled them full of groceries and canned alcohol and used our 23 kg of check on luggage allowance each to transport the food and drinks for our stay for free. If you don’t have contacts in Perth then bring empty suitcases with you or get some at an op shop or just grab some boxes. There’s a Woolworths next to the airport. There’s a supermarket at Coral Bay but it’s 2-4X the price of a major city due to the transport costs.
The only negative about the unit was having smokers next to us who smoked on their veranda.
Great
Lovely caravan park at coral bay j
Nice little caravan Park

Set up a camp site which was on a nice grassy area. Wish you could turn the lights off at the bbq areas or have them on a timer as they are really bright and kept us up. Showers near the office aren’t the best and probably need a revamp as there is no spot for putting your dry clothes. Otherwise good location. Note there is some noise of construction due to new works happening.
Great location but the bathroom are soooo dirty and when someone goes in there to “clean them” they look just as bad when they leave!

Didn't enjoy this caravan park - the service, sites and facilities were incomparable to People's Park across the road.

Coral Bay is such a remote location but this caravan park is so chockers you feel like you're in the middle of a big city.

In terms of Coral Bay itself, we still love the protected bay - the kids absolutely love it. The coral in the bay has died, and you'd need to swim out further to enjoy the reef (which is still incredible!) - you can do this by swimming or kayaking out, or doing one of the tours which are really informative and enjoyable.

Wir waren vor 3 Wochen dort, für 4 Nächte.. |Der Platz war völlig überfüllt, jeder noch so kleine Platz wurde noch mit einem Camper oder Zelt belegt |Es war dadurch sehr laut, tags und nachts.|Duschen mit extrem heißen Salzwasser.|Insgesamt sind die sanitären Anlagen total veraltet und werden nicht regelmäßig gereinigt. |Auf der ganzen Anlage gibt es kein WiFi.|In der heutigen Zeit ein no go.|Die Rezeption ist samstags und sonntags nicht besetzt.|In unseren Augen ist der Platz völlig überteuert, total ungenügend.

Warning! Warning! Warning! This caravan Park is a risk to you and your families health and safety. They disregard their obligations to the duty of care of their residents. The ablution block is rarely cleaned and when it was, it was still discusstingly dirty. Maintenance is obviously not done as everything is in poor condition. No water hook up to caravans, but don't worry you can fill up for an extra cost. I will never stay here again.

Nice park, the only thing that the showers are with salt water, I understand that it is because of the difficulty of having fresh water in Coral Bay, despite that the bathrooms are well maintained. The park has a lot of shade (for those who have tent sites) there are MANY families with small children, so if you want to have a quiet stay I don't know if it is the best place since there were children screaming and crying everywhere.
Camping sympathique. Suffisant pour quelques nuits

61 AUD pro Nacht für 2 Erwachsene und 1 Kind für Stellplatz Powersite. Völlig überzogen und Touriabzocke. Positiv sind die grünen Stellplätze und die Lage….

Sand always in the shower, and the worker on there golf carts speed around and don’t all ways slow down for children, and also speed at night in the dark back to there accommodation

Siamo stati qui in camper. La locazione è ottima, praticamente fronte mare. Parcheggio sull' erba verdissima e curata. Ci sono tutti i servizi,  bar, panetteria,  negozi e supermercato ben fornito. Siamo stati qui 2 notti e 3 gg. I bagni non mi sembra siano stati puliti.  E neanche la cucina. È un po' un problema l'acqua dolce potabile. Oltre i venti litri totali è a pagamento. Ma cu soni dei rubinetti per cucinare e bere gratuiti.

Lage super. Preise völlig überzogen. Hatten eine „Villa“. Preis pro Nacht lag bei 163 € …. Für den Preis erwarte ich aber auch Leistung. Es gab noch nicht einmal WLAN! Man konnte sich in den auf dem Platz vorhandenen Pub setzen und dort surfen… nun ja…

Very expensive caravan park, especially in high season. Unpowered site narrow and in the sand. Dirty and poorly equipped outdoor kitchen. Showers with salt water and only hot water. No WIFI. Only 4G. Washing machine expensive (6$) and no option. The caravan park looks like a usine, so many people.
Ordentliche Anlage  mit guter Lage. Zimmer sind teuer aber freundliches, hilfsbereites Personal. Leider ist es sehr laut, da Isolierung fehlt.

Gut geeignet für ein paar Tage Baden und Schnorchel Ausflüge

Stayed at Ningaloo Coral Bay Bayview in the overflow campground due to full bookings. The grassy area was a pleasant surprise, offering a decent alternative. The night was peaceful, and despite the check-in restriction after 3 pm, the 39 available sites provided a comfortable stay.

Hatten einen Stellplatz ohne Strom.

We have been staying in Bayview for our honeymoon we are in baylodge and it's amazing we have a balcony room the room is absolutely amazing  we thank the housekeeper so much for changing our bedding yesterday and gave hubby and I fresh towels the reception staff are absolutely lovely I highly recommend bayview as your holiday resort ❤️

Lodge rooms are getting a bit tired and not very soundproof. Location is great. Pool was lovely and warm.

Stayed on the overflow area #275.  Very poor and overpriced for the nothing you get, unpowered and no water. The toilets and showers and camp kitchen are filthy and all night we had the backpackers partying and coming over to use the toilets and showers as anyone can come in!  Definitely not worth the over price you pay!  Recommend you do what everyone else does and park on side of road for free and just help yourself to the filthy amenities.
Got shoved in the back in overflow area. Mob of people party till 2 am. For all the security this place has amazing that nothing got done... waste of money should just free camp n copp the fine.

In such a beautiful town it's such a pity the accommodation is so average.
The spots are tight and the roads are narrow. We saw many people with bigger vans struggling to get into their spots. The sites aren't defined at all, so lots of people encroaching on their neighbours. Many sites had trees in the way so awnings couldn't be put out.
Amenities needed to be cleaned more often and the whole place needed a good deep clean (laundry was terribly dirty).
In all it's a really average park despite the fact that it's expensive.

It's the only potable water spot in the little town. But you have to book with them to get it. And I don't mean to fill a tank. But a 1.5 L bottle. Learn how "hospitality" means, don't think just to the money.

Stayed 1 night in an unpowered site, we were packed in like sardines on the tiniest sites ever . Why would you bother ?? Had a  dog with us found there was nowhere to take him off lead.
Wouldn't recommend Coral Bay at all, beautiful coast line but overcrowded and over touristed.

Had a nice stay at the park. Checked in late morning and was fine. Sites aren’t big but can fit a van and awning. The roads in the park are narrow and can be tricky getting in and out. Showers/toilets were clean.
Very clean facilities,large pitch
Beautiful rooms with an excellent view

Stayed on a powered site,  the park was comfortable,  amenities were clean and adequate,  can't hook up to water so that is painful but had drinking water taps so that was helpful and no TV reception.
Crowed when we were there on a Thursday. Water from the shower is salty so I did not shower.

18 Zimmer auf einem Campingplatz. Coral Bay ist ein reines Touristendorf am Ende der Welt, Strand pur. Sauberes, geräumiges Zimmer mit leichten Gebrauchsspuren und Terrasse oder Balkon.||Von den Nachbarn bekommt man wenig mit.||Kein WLAN.
